underlying: Massachusetts U.S. Senate 2026 D-R margin in pp (Markey D incumbent)
method: Anchored on (a) Markey's 2020 +33.4 margin over O'Connor, (b) MA hasn't elected a Republican senator since 2010 (Brown), (c) UMass/WCVB poll (April 2026) shows Markey at 62% vs generic R, lead ~24pp, (d) Markey leads in primary (vs Moulton/Rikleen) so likely to be nominee, (e) Modest pull toward primary-poll mark (24pp) accepting some erosion since 2020. Mu=+33 anchors Markey's last general-election performance. Sigma 7 to allow downside if he draws a real moderate-R challenger like Charlie Baker (Baker has not declared but is mentioned in RCP polling).
notes: Markey 2020: 66.2 vs 33.0 (+33.4). UMass April 2026 poll shows him at 62% (~+24pp) vs generic R. There's tail risk if Charlie Baker enters (RCP shows polling Markey vs Baker), which could compress margin to ~10pp. Sigma 7 is wider than other safe-D races for that reason. Floor strikes start at P23 — even a ~25pp Markey win clears that.
